[It] Running the Cluster API E2E tests API Version Upgrade upgrade from an old version of v1beta1 to current, and scale AKS workload clusters created in the old version Should create a management cluster and then upgrade all the providers

ai_summary.summary: Cluster API Provider Azure (CAPZ) E2E test failed during clusterctl upgrade due to azureserviceoperator-controller-manager deployment not becoming ready

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test failure occurred because the azureserviceoperator-controller-manager deployment in the capz-system namespace did not become ready within the 5-minute timeout during the clusterctl upgrade process. The kube-apiserver logs from the management cluster show repeated 'dial tcp 10.100.16.220:443: connect: connection refused' errors when trying to reach the Azure Service Operator webhook service. This indicates that the azureserviceoperator-controller-manager pods were not able to start properly or the webhook service endpoint was unreachable. The controller manager logs show it was actively reconciling Azure resources and attempting to delete resources, but the deployment itself was not becoming ready. This is a persistent failure in the Azure Service Operator deployment rather than a transient infrastructure issue.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Investigate why the azureserviceoperator-controller-manager deployment is failing to become ready. Check the pod logs and events for the azureserviceoperator-controller-manager pods in the capz-system namespace on the management cluster. Verify that the webhook service is correctly configured and that the pods can start without errors. This may involve checking resource requirements, RBAC permissions, or image pull issues for the Azure Service Operator deployment.
